headline: Generative image techniques and open-weight coding models led the week as diffusion tooling and free coders entered circulation.
body: Text-to-image generation broke out alongside diffusion internals including noise prediction, layer normalization, and residual connections, with Krea 2 arriving as an open-source local image generator supporting LoRA fine-tuning and ComfyUI deployment, and DiffusionBench launching to evaluate generative diffusion transformers. Coding models also surged: GLM-5.2 appeared as a free open-weight stealth coder inside OpenCode offering roughly 200 requests per five-hour window for repo exploration, bug fixes, and refactoring. Security and adoption friction ran underneath the technique gains, with agentic AI flagged for prompt injection and data deletion risks and a Broward school board pausing an educator AI platform over privacy concerns. The mix points to a window weighted toward generative image research and open coding tooling rather than a single dominant release.
